title: Helix (EvidenceOps) Organization Blueprint (1)
normalized_content:
Helix (EvidenceOps) Organization Blueprint
Fictional Enterprise for Quality Systems Testing
1. Company Profile
Legal Name: Aetheris BioPharma Private Limited
Brand Name: Aetheris Biopharma
Industry: Pharmaceutical Manufacturing (Small-molecule oral solid dosage forms + sterile
injectables)
Headquarters: Hyderabad, Telangana, India
Year Established: 2011
Ownership: Privately held (founder-led, with institutional investors)
Employee Count: ~1,150 (as of 2026)
Primary Markets: India, Southeast Asia, Middle East, select EU generic markets
Aetheris is a mid-sized Indian pharma company with WHO-GMP, India Schedule M, and
EU-GMP certified facilities. It manufactures finished pharmaceutical products (FPPs) and
active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) for internal use and third-party contracts.
2. Mission
“To deliver high-quality, affordable medicines through science-led manufacturing, rigorous
compliance, and continuous improvement—ensuring every dose meets the highest standards
of safety, efficacy, and reliability.”
3. Business Overview
Aetheris operates two manufacturing sites in India, focusing on:
● Oral solid dosage (OSD) products (tablets, capsules)

● Sterile injectables (vials, prefilled syringes)
● API synthesis for internal formulations and select external customers
Revenue streams:
● Branded generics in domestic market
● Contract manufacturing for multinational pharma
● Exports of regulated and semi-regulated products
4. Manufacturing Sites
Site 1: Aetheris Unit I – OSD & API Complex
● Location: Pashamylaram, Telangana
● Primary Function: API synthesis + OSD formulation
● Certifications: WHO-GMP, India Schedule M, ISO 9001:2015
● Key Products: Cardiovascular, anti-diabetic, CNS tablets/capsules
Site 2: Aetheris Unit II – Sterile Injectables
● Location: Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh
● Primary Function: Sterile liquid fills (vials, PFS)
● Certifications: WHO-GMP, India Schedule M, ISO 13485 (for combination products)
● Key Products: Injectable antibiotics, oncology supports, vitamins
5. Organizational Structure
Board of Directors
↓
Managing Director (MD)
↓

● Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
● Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
● Chief Operating Officer (COO)
● Chief Quality Officer (CQO)
● Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
6. Departments
● Quality Assurance (QA): QA Operations, QC, QA Systems, Vendor Quality, CSV,
Document Control
● Quality Control (QC): Chemistry, Microbiology, Stability, Analytical R&D support
● Manufacturing Operations: OSD, API, Sterile Injectables, Packaging
● Engineering \& Utilities: Maintenance, Automation, Calibration, Utilities (HVAC, WFI,
compressed air)
● Regulatory Affairs (RA): Domestic, International, Labeling
● Supply Chain: Procurement, Warehousing, Logistics, Cold Chain
● EHS (Environment, Health \& Safety)
● IT \& Digital Systems: ERP, LIMS, MES, Infrastructure, Data Governance
● HR \& Training
● Pharmacovigilance \& Medical Affairs

14. Customer Segments
● Domestic branded generic market (hospitals, retail chains)
● Government tenders (state health schemes)
● Export distributors in SEA/Middle East
● Multinational pharma partners (contract manufacturing)
● Institutional buyers (large hospital chains)
15. Facility Layout Overview
Pashamylaram (Unit I)
● Block A: OSD manufacturing (granulation, compression, coating)
● Block B: Capsule manufacturing
● Block C: Packaging (blister, cartoning)
● API Block: API synthesis, isolation, drying
● QC Block: Chemistry, stability chambers
● Utilities: AHU, WFI, chilled water, compressed air
Visakhapatnam (Unit II)
● Sterile Block 1: Vial washing, filling, stoppering, lyophilization
● Sterile Block 2: PFS filling, assembly
● Packaging Hall: Labeling, aggregation, dispatch
● QC Block: Microbiology, sterility testing
● Utilities: WFI, clean steam, HVAC (Grade A/B/C/D)

16. Quality Management System Overview
Aetheris operates an integrated QMS aligned to:
● India Schedule M
● WHO-GMP
● EU-GMP (for exported products)
● ISO 9001:2015
● ISO 13485:2016 (for sterile injectables and combination products)
Core QMS modules:
● Document Control
● Training Management
● Deviation/OOS/OOT Management
● CAPA Management
● Internal & External Audit Management
● Supplier Qualification
● Change Control
● Complaint Handling
● Product Quality Review (PQR)
● Risk Management (ICH Q9 principles)
● Self-Inspection & Mock Audits
● Data Integrity Oversight (ALCOA+ principles)
17. Regulatory Standards Followed
● India: Drugs & Cosmetics Act, Schedule M, CDSCO guidelines
● International: WHO-GMP, EU-GMP (selected products), ICH Q7/Q8/Q9/Q10
● Quality Systems: ISO 9001, ISO 13485

● Data Integrity: 21 CFR Part 11-aligned practices (for electronic systems)
● EHS: Local PCB/SPCB norms, ISO 14001-aligned practices (in progress)
18. Document Numbering Conventions
General Documents:
[Site]-[Dept]-[Type]-[Seq]-[Rev]
● Site: PASH, VISK, CORP
● Dept: QA, QC, MFG, ENG, RA, SCM, EHS, HR, IT
● Type: POL (Policy), SOP, FRM (Form), TMP (Template), RPT (Report)
● Seq: 4-digit sequential number
● Rev: R0, R1, R2, etc.
Examples:
● PASH-QA-SOP-0101-R3
● VISK-QC-FRM-0205-R1
● CORP-QA-POL-0001-R2
19. SOP Numbering Conventions
SOPs are a subset of general documents but follow a structured area code:
[Site]-[Area]-SOP-[AreaCode]-[Seq]-[Rev]
● Area codes:
○ QA = Quality Assurance
○ QC = Quality Control
○ OSD = Oral Solid Dosage

○ API = API Manufacturing
○ ST = Sterile Injectables
○ ENG = Engineering
○ WH = Warehouse
○ RA = Regulatory Affairs
Examples:
● PASH-QA-SOP-QA-0101-R4 – Control of Documents
● PASH-OSD-SOP-OSD-0205-R2 – Tablet Compression Operation
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0301-R3 – Vial Filling Operation
● VISK-QC-SOP-QC-0112-R1 – HPLC Operation and Calibration
20. Investigation Numbering Conventions
Investigations cover deviations, OOS, OOT, complaints, and data integrity events.
[Site]-[Type]-[YYYY]-[Seq]
● Types:
○ DEV = Deviation
○ OOS = Out of Specification
○ OOT = Out of Trend
○ CMP = Complaint
○ DI = Data Integrity
○ AE = Adverse Event
Examples:
● PASH-DEV-2026-0142
● VISK-OOS-2026-0037
● PASH-CMP-2026-0089

● VISK-DI-2026-0003
21. CAPA Numbering Conventions
[Site]-CAPA-[YYYY]-[Seq]
● Site: PASH, VISK, CORP
● Seq: 4-digit sequential number starting from 0001 each year
Examples:
● PASH-CAPA-2026-0112
● VISK-CAPA-2026-0045
● CORP-CAPA-2026-0008
22. Equipment Naming Conventions
Equipment ID: EQ-[Area]-[Line]-[Seq]
● Area: OSD, API, ST, PKG, UTL, QC
● Line: 01, 02, 03 (or 00 for standalone)
● Seq: 3-digit sequential
Naming Pattern in Text:
[Area] [Equipment Type] [Line/Function] [Seq]
– – –
Example:
● ID: EQ-OSD-01-015
● Name: OSD Tablet Compression Machine Line 01 015
– – –

23. Product Naming Conventions
Internal Product Code: [Category]-[Seq]
● Category:
○ FPP = Finished Pharmaceutical Product
○ API = Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient
Example:
● FPP-1001 – Aethcard 10 mg Tablet
● API-101 – Aetherol
Brand Names:
● Cardiovascular: Aethcard
● Anti-diabetic: Glucontrol
● CNS: Neurocalm
● Injectables: Injectra, Oncosup, Vitainject
24. Batch Numbering Conventions
FPP Batches:
[SiteCode][ProductCode][YY][MM][Seq]
● SiteCode: P, V
● ProductCode: 4 digits (e.g., 1001)
● YYMM: Year and month of manufacture
● Seq: 3-digit batch sequence

Examples:
● P10012602001 – Aethcard 10 mg, PASH, Feb 2026, 1st batch
● V20012605012 – Injectra 1 g, VISK, May 2026, 12th batch
API Batches:
API-[3-digit API][YY][MM][Seq]
● Example: API-1012603002 – Aetherol, March 2026, 2nd batch
25. Investigation & CAPA Workflow Overview (High
Level)
● Event detected → Logged in respective module (Deviation/OOS/Complaint/DI)
● Preliminary assessment → Risk classification (Critical/Major/Minor)
● Investigation assigned (owner, due date)
● Root cause analysis (5 Whys, Fishbone, as applicable)
● CAPA initiation (if required)
● Implementation of actions
● Effectiveness check (defined metrics, time window)
● Closure with QA approval
26. Data & Systems Landscape
● ERP: SAP-based (finance, procurement, inventory)
● LIMS: LabWare-style system for QC data
● MES: For OSD and sterile lines (batch execution, eBR)
● QMS: Validated enterprise QMS for deviations, CAPA, change control, complaints,
audits
● DMS: Electronic document management with workflow

● LMS: Learning management system for training
● CSV Approach: GAMP 5-aligned lifecycle for all GxP systems
27. Cultural & Operational Principles
● “Quality first” as a non-negotiable value
● Strong emphasis on data integrity (ALCOA+)
● Regular mock inspections and internal audits
● Cross-functional investigation teams for major events
● Management review of QMS metrics quarterly
● Continuous improvement culture (Lean + Six Sigma projects in manufacturing)
